articles.title: UK weather: When will the heatwave end? - BBC
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
- Published
There is an end in sight to the UK's record-breaking heat after a new national temperature record for May was set on Tuesday.
For a second day in a row, the temperature exceeded 34C in south-east England and a new May record of 35.1C was recorded at Kew Gardens in London.
It follows a remarkable few days of searing heat that saw six amber heat health alerts issued across south-west England, the West Midlands, East Midlands, east of England, south east and London regions.
Monday and Tuesday saw nearly all weather stations across England and Wales break their local temperature records for May.
When will the heat ease off?
Cooler air will move in across northern and eastern of areas of England on Wednesday and bring a break to the heatwave.
However, the heat will remain across Wales, the West Country and south-west England and still likely in heatwave threshold.
On Wednesday, with cooler air spreading in across northern England, the Midlands and eastern England some locations will see a 10-degree drop in temperature.
Maximums will be around 19-23C.
Though London and south-east England will still get temperatures up to 27C, it won't be 28C needed to officially continue the heatwave.
However, Wales and south-western England will remain fairly hot, away from the cooler air moving in. Highs here could still reach 30C.
By Thursday, the supply of cooler air will be cut off as hot air re-surges from France once again. This means that temperatures will increase, with 30C back on the cards.
Most weather forecast models show that cooler weather will gradually come in again from Friday and into the weekend.
The jet stream, currently to the north of us, will wobble back southwards, meaning areas of low pressure, occasional rain, stronger winds and a drop in temperature as we head into the first week of June.
How extreme has this heatwave been?
Meteorologically speaking, the UK is still in spring.
And with temperatures across many parts of the UK ranging from 10 to 15 degrees above normal, this early heatwave is unprecedented.
Ed Hawkins, professor of climate science at the University of Reading told the BBC that this heatwave "stands out".
Since record-keeping began in the UK, there have only been a handful of years when temperatures reached or exceeded 30C in May.
Within this May heatwave however most of England and parts of Wales has seen temperatures soar above 30C for more than a couple of days in places.
Values in the mid-30s were once uncommon in the British Isles even at the height of summer.
With climate change we've seen average temperatures rise and this means "today's heat events are emerging earlier, intensifying faster and occurring across a much warmer background climate", Prof Hawkins said.

articles.title: Uttarakhand cop helps thirsty monkey drink water from his own hands amid heatwave - India Today
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
Uttarakhand cop helps thirsty monkey drink water from his own hands amid heatwave
An Uttarakhand Police officer was filmed giving water to an exhausted monkey during a severe heatwave. The video has sharpened attention to how extreme summer temperatures are affecting animals and birds.
Amid rising temperatures and relentless summer heat across north India, a small act of kindness by an Uttarakhand Police officer is winning hearts online after he was seen offering water to a thirsty monkey on the roadside.
The viral video, shared online by Uttarakhand Police, captures the calm moment when the police officer notices the visibly exhausted monkey struggling in the scorching heat. Instead of shooing the animal away, the cop gently offers it water using a bottle while the monkey patiently drinks beside him.
The touching interaction quickly struck an emotional chord on social media, with many users calling it a reminder that animals too suffer silently during brutal summer conditions.
The clip was filmed amid intense heatwave conditions currently affecting several parts of north India, including Uttarakhand. With temperatures soaring, incidents of dehydrated animals and birds desperately searching for water have increasingly surfaced online in recent weeks.
Watch the video:
Social media users praised the officer’s compassion, saying small gestures like these matter deeply during extreme weather conditions. Several people also urged others to leave bowls of water outside homes and public spaces for birds and stray animals struggling in the heat.
The video has also reignited conversations around how rising temperatures are affecting wildlife and urban animals alike. Recently, several heartbreaking clips have gone viral showing monkeys, birds and stray animals collapsing or desperately searching for water amid severe heatwaves across India.
Many users online described the moment as “humanity at its simplest”, while others pointed out that kindness towards animals often says a lot about society itself.
As the monkey quietly drank water from the officer’s hands, the video became more than just another viral clip. For many viewers online, it served as a gentle reminder that compassion sometimes appears in the smallest acts — especially during the harshest summers.

articles.title: Uttarakhand Police Officer Assists Thirsty Monkey During Heatwave - The CSR Journal
articles.full_text:
In a display of compassion, a police officer from Uttarakhand was filmed offering water to a thirsty monkey, touching the hearts of many online. Amidst the soaring temperatures and oppressive heatwave affecting northern India, the scene showcased a moment of humanity. The video, shared by Uttarakhand Police, shows the officer noticing the visibly distressed monkey and opting to help rather than ignore the animal’s plight.
The video captures the officer gently providing water using a bottle, while the monkey patiently quenches its thirst beside him. This act of kindness quickly gained attention on social media, resonating with users who appreciated the empathy displayed towards wildlife during particularly harsh weather conditions.
Increased Incidence of Animal Dehydration
The incident has occurred amid a severe heatwave that has been affecting various regions in northern India, including Uttarakhand. As temperatures continue to rise, reports of dehydrated animals and birds in need of water have become more frequent. The viral video serves as a testament to the importance of looking after local wildlife in such extreme weather scenarios.
Many social media users have expressed concern about animals suffering from the heat, urging communities to take action. Suggestions have emerged for people to place bowls of water in accessible areas for stray animals and birds. Such initiatives highlight a collective responsibility to provide care for those who share the environment with us.
This recent event forms part of a broader trend, with several social media clips emerging that show animals in distress due to the ongoing heatwave. These visuals have sparked discussions about the dire situations faced by wildlife and the need for greater awareness and action on their behalf.
Broader Impact on Society’s Perception of Wildlife
The officer’s interaction with the monkey has resonated deeply with observers, leading many to reflect on humanity’s role in safeguarding animals. Comments on social media describe the moment as emblematic of “humanity at its simplest,” emphasising the significance of small acts of kindness. The incident has prompted many to consider how these gestures may reflect a larger societal attitude towards compassion and care for all living beings.
As people engage with the video, it has become more than just a popular clip; it serves as a reminder of the ongoing challenges faced by wildlife amid climate changes. The growing awareness around animal welfare during extreme weather is crucial, as incidents involving suffering animals gain more visibility on platforms like social media.
This heartwarming display of kindness by the Uttarakhand police officer brings forth essential conversations about compassion and awareness towards animals, particularly during times of distress. The hope remains that such acts inspire others to extend their support to vulnerable creatures in desperate need of help, reinforcing the societal responsibility towards wildlife preservation.

articles.title: Uttrakhand में भीषण गर्मी का कहर, IMD ने लू की चेतावनी जारी की - totaltv.in
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
Uttrakhand इस समय भीषण गर्मी की चपेट में है; पूरे राज्य में तापमान लगातार बढ़ता जा रहा है। गर्मी के इस भीषण कहर को देखते हुए मौसम विभाग ने अगले 48 घंटों के लिए मैदानी इलाकों और उनसे सटे पहाड़ी जिलों के लिए लू (हीटवेव) की चेतावनी जारी की है। हालांकि, अगामी कुछ दिनों में राहत की उम्मीद की जा सकती है। Uttrakhand

मौसम वैज्ञानिकों का कहना है कि गुरुवार से एक ‘पश्चिमी विक्षोभ’ (Western Disturbance) सक्रिय होने की संभावना है, जिससे राज्य के कई हिस्सों में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश हो सकती है। राजधानी देहरादून में, ‘नौतपा’—गर्मी का वह नौ-दिनों का दौर जिसे सबसे भीषण माना जाता है—शुरू हो चुका है; ऐसे में दिन के समय घर से बाहर निकलना निवासियों के लिए बेहद मुश्किल होता जा रहा है।
जैसे-जैसे गर्मी बढ़ रही है, लोग घरों के अंदर ही रहने के लिए मजबूर हैं और ठंडक पाने के लिए पारंपरिक तरीकों का भी सहारा ले रहे हैं । हालांकि इस सप्ताह के अंत में होने वाली संभावित बारिश से कुछ राहत मिल सकती है, लेकिन मौसम विभाग ने राज्य के कुछ हिस्सों में आंधी-तूफान, ओलावृष्टि, बिजली गिरने और तेज़ हवाएं चलने की भी चेतावनी दी है, और निवासियों से सतर्क रहने का आग्रह किया है।

articles.title: भीषण गर्मी के बीच उत्तराखंड में 5 दिन बारिश का अलर्ट; आज इन इलाकों में बदल रहा मौसम - Hindustan Hindi News
articles.full_text:
भीषण गर्मी के बीच उत्तराखंड में 5 दिन बारिश का अलर्ट; आज इन इलाकों में बदल रहा मौसम
Uttarakhand Rain Alert: भीषण गर्मी और लू के थपेड़ों के बीच मौसम विभाग ने गुड न्यूज दी है कि अगले कुछ दिन उत्तराखंड में बारिश हो सकती है। आज भी कई जगह बारिश की संभावना है।
Uttarakhand Rain Alert: उत्तराखंड में मौसम के दो रंग देखने को मिल रहे हैं। एक तरफ मैदानी जिलों में प्रचंड गर्मी और लू के थपेड़ों ने लोगों को बेहाल कर दिया, वहीं पहाड़ों पर बादलों की आवाजाही, बारिश और ठंडी हवाओं से मौसम सुहावना हो गया। मौसम विभाग ने इस बीच गुड न्यूज भी दी है। बताया कि 28 मई से देशभर में मौसम बदलने वाला है। आने वाले तीन से पांच दिनों में उत्तराखंड के मैदानी इलाकों में भी बारिश हो सकती है। आज पहाड़ों में बारिश का अलर्ट है।
मैदानों में लू का सितम, दोपहर में सन्नाटा
प्रदेश के मैदानी इलाकों में मंगलवार को सुबह से ही तेज धूप और गर्म हवाओं का दौर शुरू हो गया था। भीषण गर्मी के कारण दोपहर के समय बाजारों और सड़कों पर सन्नाटा पसरा नजर आया। हालांकि, दोपहर बाद हवाओं में थोड़ी नरमी आने से देहरादून का तापमान 40.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस से गिरकर 38.6 डिग्री दर्ज किया गया, जो कि सामान्य से तीन डिग्री अधिक है। वहीं, हरिद्वार और रुड़की में पारा 40 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच गया, जिससे लोग दिनभर उमस और तपिश से परेशान रहे।
पहाड़ों में बरसे बदरा
मैदानों से बिल्कुल उलट, पर्वतीय क्षेत्रों में दोपहर बाद मौसम का मिजाज बदल गया। सुबह खिली धूप के बाद अचानक आसमान में काले बादल छा गए। चमोली और बद्रीनाथ में तेज हवाओं के साथ हल्की बूंदाबांदी हुई। वहीं, उच्च हिमालयी क्षेत्रों, मुनस्यारी और बागेश्वर में अच्छी बारिश होने से तापमान में भारी गिरावट दर्ज की गई है। पिथौरागढ़ में 17.4, डीडीहाट में आठ, बेरीनाग में दो, गरूड़ में 1.5 एमएम बारिश दर्ज की गई। नई टिहरी में सामान्य से एक डिग्री कम तापमान 27.5 डिग्री रहा।
अगले तीन दिनों में मिलेगी गर्मी से राहत
मौसम विज्ञान केंद्र के निदेशक डॉ. सीएस तोमर के मुताबिक, बुधवार को उत्तरकाशी, रुद्रप्रयाग, चमोली, बागेश्वर और पिथौरागढ़ जिलों में हल्की बारिश की संभावना है। राज्य के बाकी जिलों में मौसम शुष्क रहेगा। अगले एक-दो दिनों में अधिकतम तापमान में कोई बड़ा बदलाव नहीं होगा, लेकिन इसके बाद अगले तीन से पांच दिनों के भीतर तापमान में तीन से पांच डिग्री तक की गिरावट आने की उम्मीद है। इससे मैदानों में भी गर्मी से कुछ राहत मिल सकती है।
पूरे देश में भीषण तपिश
पूरा देश इस समय भीषण गर्मी और लू की चपेट में है। हालांकि 28 मई से एक नया पश्चिमी विक्षोभ सक्रिय होने जा रहा है। इससे मैदानी इलाकों में भी बारिश होगी।
